main_sections.title: Badal and Moti
main_sections.prose_summary: Badal is a village boy who lives happily with his mother. One cold evening, returning from school, he finds a shivering puppy and brings it home. His mother agrees to let the puppy stay only if Badal promises to care for it, and together they name the puppy Moti. Over time, Moti grows into Badal's devoted best friend — they eat, play, and go everywhere together. The crisis arrives on a rainy day when Badal slips on a muddy lane and falls into a deep pit, unable to climb out. His worried mother gathers the neighbours to search for him; Moti joins them, catches Badal's scent, and leads everyone to the pit by barking. The neighbours use a rope to pull Badal out, and a relieved Badal hugs and thanks his loyal friend Moti. The story explores themes of responsibility, friendship, loyalty, and the bond between a child and a pet.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Answer the following.
1. What did Badal come across by the side of the road?
2. What did Badal and his mother name the puppy?
3. What did Badal fall into?
4. How did Moti find Badal?
5. Write 'T' for true and 'F' for false.
a) Badal lived with his grandmother.
b) Moti grew up and loved Badal.
c) Badal's mother was worried when he did not come home in time.
d) Moti caught Badal's photo.
e) Badal hugged his puppy Moti and thanked him.

question_bank.stem: What did Badal come across by the side of the road?
What did Badal and his mother name the puppy?
What did Badal fall into?
How did Moti find Badal?
Badal lived with his grandmother.
Moti grew up and loved Badal.
Badal's mother was worried when he did not come home in time.
Moti caught Badal's photo.
Badal hugged his puppy Moti and thanked him.

oracy.section_name: Let us think — B + Let us listen — A + Let us speak — A + Let us speak — B

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Think and discuss in pairs.
1. Do you remember when you fell down last time? How did you fall? Who picked you up? Share your experiences.
2. Do you have a pet? Do you know someone who has a pet?
3. If you were allowed to keep any animal as a pet, which animal would you choose? Why?

question_bank.stem: Do you remember when you fell down last time? How did you fall? Who picked you up? Share your experiences.
Do you have a pet? Do you know someone who has a pet?
If you were allowed to keep any animal as a pet, which animal would you choose? Why?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Your teacher will read out the story of Badal and Moti once again to you. Now read the following sentences and number them in the sequence of the story. The first sentence has already been marked for you.
One rainy day, he slipped and fell into a deep pit. ( )
Badal and his mother named the puppy Moti. ( )
Badal hugged Moti. ( )
Badal came across a puppy by the side of the road. ( 1 )
Moti accompanied Badal's mother to search for him. ( )
Moti followed Badal's scent and went near the pit and started barking. ( )

question_bank.stem: Number these story events in sequence after listening to the story read aloud: One rainy day, he slipped and fell into a deep pit / Badal and his mother named the puppy Moti / Badal hugged Moti / Moti accompanied Badal's mother to search for him / Moti followed Badal's scent and went near the pit and started barking.

tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: There was a boy called Badal. He lived with his mother in a village. One evening, returning from school, he saw a puppy shivering in the cold. Badal brought him home. His mother agreed if he promised to care for the puppy. They named him Moti. Moti grew up and they became best friends. One rainy day Badal slipped on a muddy lane and fell into a deep pit. His mother gathered the neighbours to search for him. Moti followed Badal's scent, went near the pit, and started barking. The neighbours used a rope to pull Badal out. Badal hugged and thanked Moti.

question_bank.stem: Talk in pairs: what do you do before you come to school, during school hours, and after going back home?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. You may use the following clues.
1. Every day I ___ up at ___ in the morning. Then I ___ my teeth and go to the ___. After that, I take a ___ and dress up for school. I ___ breakfast and I ___ to school.
2. ___
3. ___

question_bank.stem: Complete and say aloud: describe your daily routine before school, during school, and after school using the sentence clues provided.

word_work.section_name: Let us learn — A + Let us learn — B

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Look at the following words from the lesson.
lived  loved  played  gathered  thanked
Notice that all these words end with 'ed'. All these actions have already happened.
Add 'ed' and write:
walk + ed = ___
play ___
talk ___
ask ___
touch ___

question_bank.stem: Add 'ed' to: walk, play, talk, ask, touch.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Match the words to make pairs. Colour each pair with the same colour. Two have been done for you.
paper  ball
chair  thread
bat  and  key
needle  pencil
lock  sambar
idli  table

question_bank.stem: Match the words to make pairs (e.g. bat and ball, needle and thread): paper–pencil, chair–table, lock–key, idli–sambar.
question_bank.table:
Word 1|and|Word 2
paper| |pencil
chair| |table
bat| |ball
needle| |thread
lock| |key
idli| |sambar

writing.section_name: Let us write — A + Let us write — B + Let us write — C

tasks_verbatim.task_text: A. Look at the pictures. They show a story of two friends: a pigeon and an ant. But the pictures are jumbled up. Number them to get the correct order to make a story.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Six jumbled pictures depicting the story of a pigeon and an ant — the ant falls into a stream, the pigeon drops a leaf to save it, a hunter aims at the pigeon, and the ant bites the hunter's hand to save the pigeon.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Now fill in the blanks using the words in the box to complete the story.
leaf  flew  branch  climbed  fell  stream  hunter
A dove was resting on a ___ of a tree near a ___. An ant ___ into the stream and cried for help. The dove dropped a ___ into the stream. The ant ___ on the leaf and landed safely. Another day the ant saw a ___ aiming at the dove. She quickly climbed up and bit the hunter's hand. The dove ___ away unhurt.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blank: A dove was resting on a ___ of a tree near a ___.
Fill in the blank: An ant ___ into the stream and cried for help.
Fill in the blank: The dove dropped a ___ into the stream.
Fill in the blank: The ant ___ on the leaf and landed safely.
Fill in the blank: Another day the ant saw a ___ aiming at the dove.
Fill in the blank: She quickly climbed up and bit the hunter's hand. The dove ___ away unhurt.
Complete the full fill-in-blank passage about the dove and the ant using: leaf, flew, branch, climbed, fell, stream, hunter.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. Look at these sentences from the lesson.
"Can this puppy stay with us, Maa?"
"Yes beta, but only if you promise to take care of the puppy."
Add a ? or a . to the following:
1. The sun rises in the east ___
2. What is your name ___
3. I like to eat mangoes ___
4. May I borrow your English textbook ___
5. Brinda and Namrata are good friends ___
6. Whose shoes are these ___

tasks_verbatim.task_text: A. Look at the picture below. It shows three children playing. Would you like to add anything to this picture? Use your crayons or colour pencils to add to the picture.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Illustration of three children playing outdoors — one rolling a hoop, one tossing a ball, one on a tree swing — with space for learners to draw additional elements.
